_comment: This file documents JSONL format — actual JSONL has one JSON object per line with no wrapping array. The 'lines' array below shows each line as a separate string for readability in this fixture.
description: Shopify bulk operation JSONL output format. Each line is a complete JSON object. Nested resources (variants, metafields, line items) appear as separate objects with a __parentId field pointing to their parent's id.

flat_objects_example._comment: Bulk query with no nesting — products only, no variants. Each line is a root object.
flat_objects_example.query: { products { edges { node { id title handle vendor status } } } }

nested_objects_example._comment: Bulk query with one level of nesting — products with variants. Variants appear after their parent product with __parentId set to the product's GID.
nested_objects_example.query: { products { edges { node { id title variants { edges { node { id price sku inventoryQuantity } } } } } } }

deep_nesting_example._comment: Multi-level nesting: products → variants → metafields. Each level references its parent via __parentId.
deep_nesting_example.query: { products { edges { node { id title variants { edges { node { id sku metafields(first: 5) { edges { node { id namespace key value } } } } } } } } } }

orders_example._comment: Order export with nested line items — common use case for analytics or ERP sync.
orders_example.query: { orders { edges { node { id name totalPriceSet { shopMoney { amount currencyCode } } createdAt lineItems { edges { node { id title quantity originalUnitPrice { amount currencyCode } } } } } } } }

mutation_variables_example._comment: JSONL input for a bulk mutation — each line is a variables object for one mutation invocation. These are uploaded to the staged URL before calling bulkOperationRunMutation.
mutation_variables_example.mutation: mutation ($input: ProductVariantInput!) { productVariantUpdate(input: $input) { productVariant { id price } userErrors { field message } } }

mutation_result_example._comment: JSONL output from a completed bulk mutation — each line is the result of one mutation invocation. Check userErrors per line for individual failures.

format_rules._comment: Key rules for JSONL format in Shopify bulk operations
rules: Each line is exactly one complete JSON object — no multi-line JSON
Lines are separated by a newline character (\n)
Trailing newline at end of file is optional
Blank lines should be skipped by the parser
Nested resources appear as separate lines with __parentId set to the parent GID
The __parentId field is stripped when building the nested object structure
Parents always appear before their children in the output (parent-first ordering)
Prices and quantities may be strings or numbers depending on the field type
Result JSONL from queries and from mutations have different structures — queries return resource objects, mutations return mutation result objects
